question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a continuous stream of requests with monotonically incrementing unique numeric block numbers (e.g., 0x1, 0x2, 0x3, ...) that do not exist in the database, causing findByIndex() to execute a DB query for each unique value because the cache key is per-index and cache misses for non-existent blocks are never re-used, resulting in sustained database CPU and I/O load exceeding 30% above baseline?
